title: Fetch, Streams, AbortSignal, Conditional Requests, and Publisher Budgets · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for fetch, streams, abortsignal, conditional requests, and publisher budgets and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded publisher-friendly Fetch adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Compose caller, deadline, shutdown, and attempt signals, preserve abort reasons, and remove listeners through settled ownership.
Read response streams incrementally with wire, decoded, compression, character, time, and cancellation limits and release or cancel the body.
Send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, give If-None-Match precedence, handle 304 without a body, and commit validators only with admitted state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Feed Ts Fetch Signal Composition · changed case · Quiz Feed Ts Fetc…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 7597 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Fetch stream limits, signal composition, validators, redirect policy, and publisher budgets; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compose caller, deadline, shutdown, and attempt signals, preserve abort reasons, and remove listeners through settled ownership.
